title: (BUG) relay/main: plaintext WebSocket with no TLS termination documented
description: relay/src/main.rs binds a plain axum HTTP/WebSocket listener on LISTEN_ADDR (default 0.0.0.0:3210) with no TLS. The relay is documented as requiring a TLS-terminating reverse proxy (nginx/caddy) in front of it, but this is not enforced or even warned about at startup. If an operator exposes the relay directly (e.g., on a cloud VM without a proxy), all relay traffic is plaintext even though individual messages are E2E-encrypted by MLS. The relay's authentication nonce, connection metadata, and IP addresses travel in cleartext. Fix: add a startup warning when LISTEN_ADDR is not a loopback address and TLS is not configured (e.g., via a BEHIND_PROXY=true env var acknowledgement), similar to how daemon/src/main.rs enforces loopback-only binding.
